lines: Yesterday's headline: Portugal got mugged by Spain, sending Butters' star man home 0–1 and handing Jim (5th, 59pts) a massive boost. Jim was doing nothing with Spain for weeks and now suddenly he's relevant. Typical.
Belgium absolutely battered USA 4–1, but since neither side belongs to anyone here, we move swiftly on. Noted. Forgotten.

Today's first order of business: Argentina vs Egypt. Ellis (last place, 38pts) needs her last surviving team to win — yes, Argentina is literally all she has left. No pressure, Ellis, you absolute fuck, but if Messi's lads slip up you're basically a spectator for the rest of the tournament.

The real drama is Switzerland vs Colombia — Carter (4th, 67pts) AND The Foreman (8th, 44pts) both have Colombia, meaning it's a shared payday if they go through, or a shared disaster if Switzerland park the bus and ruin everyone's afternoon.

Watch out for Colombia today — if they win, Carter closes in on the top three and The Foreman stops being an embarrassment, but if Switzerland nick it, those two will have to sit and watch Callum, Wilmot and Butters carve up the rest of the tournament between them with their Norway-Morocco double act.
